channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Building Latino Leadership: Career Opportunities at the Congressional Hispanic Caucus Institute (CHCI)
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Congressional Hispanic Caucus Institute (CHCI) stands as the nation’s premier Hispanic nonprofit leadership development organization, dedicated to cultivating the next generation of Latino leaders. With a powerful mission rooted in equity, opportunity, and representation, CHCI continues to expand its impact through transformative programs, strategic partnerships, and a growing team of passionate professionals. This article […]

The post Building Latino Leadership: Career Opportunities at the Congressional Hispanic Caucus Institute (CHCI) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Congressional Hispanic Caucus Institute (CHCI) stands as the nation’s premier Hispanic nonprofit leadership development organization, dedicated to cultivating the next generation of Latino leaders. With a powerful mission rooted in equity, opportunity, and representation, CHCI continues to expand its impact through transformative programs, strategic partnerships, and a growing team of passionate professionals. This article provides a comprehensive overview of CHCI’s work, its organizational growth, its commitment to diversity, and the exciting career opportunities currently available.

About CHCI: A National Leader in Latino Leadership Development

CHCI has built a strong reputation as a leading nonprofit organization focused on empowering Latino youth and emerging leaders. Through its award‑winning leadership development programs, CHCI provides pathways for academic advancement, professional growth, and civic engagement. The organization’s work is grounded in the belief that investing in Latino leadership strengthens communities, institutions, and the nation as a whole.

Over the past decade, CHCI has undergone significant transformation, expanding its reach, programs, and organizational capacity. Key highlights include:

  • Budget Growth: CHCI has grown its annual budget to $8 million, enabling the expansion of programs and services.
  • Staff Expansion: The organization now employs approximately 30 dedicated professionals who support its mission-driven work.
  • Student Impact: CHCI directly impacts more than 1,700 students annually through its leadership programs.
  • Alumni Network: With over 4,000 accomplished alumni, CHCI has cultivated a powerful network of leaders across the public, private, and nonprofit sectors.

This growth reflects CHCI’s commitment to increasing access, opportunity, and representation for the Latino community nationwide.

Commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

CHCI upholds a strong commitment to equal employment opportunities and inclusive leadership. The organization prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind, ensuring a safe, equitable, and welcoming environment for all employees and program participants.

CHCI’s non-discrimination policy covers a wide range of protected characteristics, including:

  • Race, color, and national origin
  • Religion, belief, or spirituality
  • Age, gender, gender identity, or expression
  • Sexual orientation
  • Disability and genetic information
  • Veteran status
  • Marital or family status
  • Pregnancy and related conditions
  • Political affiliation
  • Personal appearance
  • Credit information
  • Any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws

This policy applies to all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, training, and workplace culture.

Employee Benefits at CHCI

CHCI offers a competitive and comprehensive benefits package designed to support the well‑being and professional growth of its full‑time staff. Benefits include:

  1. Medical and Dental Insurance
  2. 401(k) Plan with Company Match
  3. 15 Days of Vacation and Sick Leave Upon Hire
  4. Transportation Subsidy
  5. Additional Benefits Supporting Work-Life Balance and Career Development

These benefits reflect CHCI’s commitment to attracting and retaining exceptional talent dedicated to advancing Latino leadership.

Current Open Positions at CHCI

CHCI is currently hiring for multiple roles across various departments. All positions are based in Washington, DC, with hybrid work options available.

Development and Corporate Relations

  • Junior Account Manager, Development and Corporate Relations – Washington, DC (Hybrid)
  • Manager, Development and Corporate Relations – Washington, DC (Hybrid)

Executive

  • Government Relations Intern – Washington, DC (Hybrid)

Finance & Administration

  • Staff Accountant – Washington, DC (Hybrid)

Strategic Events

  • Strategic Events Coordinator – Washington, DC (Hybrid)

These roles offer an opportunity to contribute to CHCI’s mission while working in a dynamic, mission-driven environment.

CHCI’s Broader Impact and Community Engagement

Beyond its leadership programs and career opportunities, CHCI actively engages the broader community through initiatives such as:

  • CHCI Podcast: Celebrating the accomplishments of American Latinos.
  • Newsletter Subscription: Keeping supporters informed about programs, events, and opportunities.
  • Community Giving: Encouraging donations that help strengthen the Latino community for future generations.

CHCI emphasizes that contributions are investments in long-term community empowerment.

 

For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here

Discover more job opportunities here 

 

The post Building Latino Leadership: Career Opportunities at the Congressional Hispanic Caucus Institute (CHCI) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CYD Proof of Concept Fellowships 2026 in Switzerland | Up to CHF 130,000 for Cyber-Defence Innovators and Entrepreneurs
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship Program 2026 is now accepting applications from talented innovators, researchers, and future entrepreneurs with groundbreaking ideas in cyber-defence and security technologies. Jointly launched by the EPFL Research Office and the Cyber-Defence (CYD) Campus in Switzerland, this prestigious fellowship supports applied research projects that aim to develop proof-of-concept solutions capable […]

The post CYD Proof of Concept Fellowships 2026 in Switzerland | Up to CHF 130,000 for Cyber-Defence Innovators and Entrepreneurs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship Program 2026 is now accepting applications from talented innovators, researchers, and future entrepreneurs with groundbreaking ideas in cyber-defence and security technologies.

Jointly launched by the EPFL Research Office and the Cyber-Defence (CYD) Campus in Switzerland, this prestigious fellowship supports applied research projects that aim to develop proof-of-concept solutions capable of addressing real-world cyber-security challenges.

The program is part of the broader CYD Fellowships initiative and is specifically designed to help innovators transform research ideas into practical products or services with strong commercialization potential.

With funding of up to CHF 130,000 per project, the fellowship offers a unique opportunity for researchers, startups, and technology entrepreneurs from around the world to work in Switzerland’s advanced cyber-defence ecosystem.

About the CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship

The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship is a specialized research and innovation initiative focused on strengthening cyber-defence capabilities through applied scientific and technological development.

The fellowship encourages applicants to build innovative solutions that can contribute to:

  • Cyber-security enhancement
  • Digital trust technologies
  • AI-driven cyber-defence systems
  • Data science for security applications
  • Privacy-preserving technologies
  • Information-sharing systems
  • Cyber-physical system protection

Selected fellows will conduct their projects at the CYD Campus facilities located within the campuses of EPFL and ETH Zurich, as well as at the CYD headquarters in Thun, Switzerland.

The program is ideal for entrepreneurs and researchers who want to bridge the gap between academic research and real-world cyber-security solutions.

Fellowship Objectives

The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship aims to:

  1. Support innovative cyber-defence research projects
  2. Encourage entrepreneurship in cyber-security
  3. Develop practical proof-of-concept technologies
  4. Promote commercialization of research outcomes
  5. Strengthen Swiss cyber-defence capabilities
  6. Foster collaboration between academia, startups, and innovation sectors

The program seeks highly motivated applicants capable of transforming innovative ideas into scalable and impactful security solutions.

Areas of Research and Innovation

Projects submitted under the CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship must address at least one of the following cyber-defence themes:

1. Cyber and Cyber-Physical System Protection

Projects focused on improving:

  • Confidentiality
  • Integrity
  • Availability
  • Resilience of digital infrastructures

2. Information Sharing and Data Fusion

Research involving:

  • Secure information-sharing systems
  • Crowdsourcing technologies
  • Collaborative cyber-intelligence platforms
  • Threat intelligence fusion systems

3. Data Science and AI for Cyber-Defence

Innovations using:

  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Machine Learning
  • Predictive analytics
  • Big data analysis
  • Cyber-threat detection systems

4. Security, Privacy, and Digital Trust Technologies

Projects involving:

  • Privacy-enhancing technologies
  • Encryption systems
  • Identity management
  • Digital trust frameworks
  • Secure authentication systems

Fellowship Duration

The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship is awarded for a duration of:

  • 12 months

By the end of the fellowship period, fellows are expected to deliver a working proof of concept demonstrating the feasibility and potential impact of their innovation.

Funding and Financial Support

The fellowship provides substantial financial support to selected applicants.

Maximum Funding Amount

  • Up to CHF 130,000 per project

Eligible Costs

Funding may cover:

  • Personnel expenses
  • Development costs
  • Research-related expenses
  • Technical implementation costs
  • Prototype development
  • Innovation and testing activities

The funding is specifically intended for costs directly linked to the realization of the proposed project.

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the official application guidelines for detailed information on eligible expenditures.

Who Can Apply?

The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship welcomes applicants from around the world.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Applicants of any nationality are eligible
  • No age restrictions apply
  • Applicants must hold or currently pursue:
    • A Bachelor’s degree
    • A Master’s degree
    • Or a Doctoral degree recognized in Switzerland
  • Applicants must be hosted by:
    • A Swiss higher education research center
    • Their own startup based in Switzerland
    • Or EPFL Innovation Park

This fellowship is especially suitable for:

  • Cyber-security researchers
  • AI innovators
  • Startup founders
  • Technology entrepreneurs
  • Data scientists
  • Privacy and digital trust specialists

Host Institutions and Research Environment

The CYD Fellowship provides access to one of Europe’s leading innovation ecosystems.

Main Institutions Involved

  • EPFL (École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne)
  • CYD Campus
  • ETH Zurich
  • armasuisse Science and Technology

Participants benefit from:

  • Advanced research infrastructure
  • Access to cyber-defence experts
  • Collaboration opportunities
  • Entrepreneurial mentoring
  • Exposure to innovation and commercialization networks

Application Process

The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship uses a competitive two-stage application process.

Stage 1: Pre-Proposal Evaluation

Applications are first reviewed by an internal CYD Campus committee.

Evaluation criteria include:

  • Applicant suitability
  • Alignment with CYD Campus goals
  • Innovation quality
  • Relevance to cyber-defence priorities

Mentor Interview

Shortlisted candidates are assigned a prospective CYD mentor who conducts an interview.

Only applicants endorsed by their assigned mentor can proceed to the next stage.

Stage 2: Full Proposal Submission

Endorsed candidates must submit a detailed full proposal outlining:

  • Technical approach
  • Innovation potential
  • Project implementation
  • Expected outcomes
  • Commercialization opportunities

Final decisions are made by a committee chaired by the Director of the CYD Campus alongside representatives from:

  • Swiss higher education institutions
  • Innovation sectors
  • CYD Campus leadership

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit all required documents through the official online submission platform.

Important notes:

  • All documents must be submitted in English
  • Applicants should carefully review the official application toolkit and guidelines before submission

Deadline

  • Rolling applications are currently open

Since applications are accepted on a rolling basis, early submission is strongly recommended to maximize selection opportunities.

Why You Should Apply

The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship offers an exceptional platform for innovators seeking to build impactful cyber-security technologies.

Key benefits include:

  • Up to CHF 130,000 in funding
  • Access to Switzerland’s leading research institutions
  • Collaboration with cyber-defence experts
  • Entrepreneurial mentorship
  • Proof-of-concept development support
  • Commercialization opportunities
  • International networking exposure
  • Opportunity to contribute to global cyber-security advancement

For researchers and entrepreneurs passionate about cyber-defence innovation, this fellowship represents a major opportunity to transform ideas into practical, scalable solutions.

Learn more and apply

Applicants are encouraged to review the complete application guidelines and toolkit before applying.

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the CYD Proof of Concept Fellowships 2026 in Switzerland.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post CYD Proof of Concept Fellowships 2026 in Switzerland | Up to CHF 130,000 for Cyber-Defence Innovators and Entrepreneurs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary at Stellenbosch University in South Africa | R50,000 Financial Support for International Students
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
International students from the Southern African Development Community (SADC) region who are currently studying in South Africa now have an excellent opportunity to secure financial support for their academic journey through the 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary at Stellenbosch University. The bursary programme, administered by Stellenbosch University International, is designed to assist academically progressing undergraduate students […]

The post 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary at Stellenbosch University in South Africa | R50,000 Financial Support for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
International students from the Southern African Development Community (SADC) region who are currently studying in South Africa now have an excellent opportunity to secure financial support for their academic journey through the 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary at Stellenbosch University.

The bursary programme, administered by Stellenbosch University International, is designed to assist academically progressing undergraduate students who demonstrate financial need. Successful applicants will receive bursary funding valued at R50,000 to help cover study-related expenses for the 2026 academic year.

This funding initiative supports students from eligible SADC countries who are enrolled at Stellenbosch University and are continuing into their second, third, or fourth year of undergraduate study in 2026.

About the 2026 Stellenbosch University SADC Undergraduate Bursary

The 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary is specifically targeted at international undergraduate students from the Southern African Development Community (SADC) region who are currently studying at Stellenbosch University in South Africa.

The bursary aims to support deserving students who are facing financial challenges while remaining committed to completing their academic programmes successfully.

The programme recognizes the importance of increasing access to higher education for students across Southern Africa and strengthening regional academic development.

Bursary Value

Successful applicants will receive:

  • R50,000 per bursary award

The bursary funding is primarily intended to assist with:

  • Study fees
  • Academic-related costs
  • Accommodation support (where applicable through top-up funding arrangements)

Please note that the bursary cannot be used to settle historical debt.

Who Can Apply?

Applications are open to international undergraduate students from eligible SADC countries who will be in:

  • 2nd Year of study in 2026
  • 3rd Year of study in 2026
  • 4th Year of study in 2026 (for four-year degree programmes)

Eligible SADC countries include:

  1. Angola
  2. Botswana
  3. Comoros
  4. Democratic Republic of Congo (DR Congo)
  5. Lesotho
  6. Madagascar
  7. Malawi
  8. Mauritius
  9. Mozambique
  10. Namibia
  11. Seychelles
  12. Eswatini
  13. Tanzania
  14. Zambia
  15. Zimbabwe

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for consideration, applicants must meet several important criteria.

Financial Need

Applicants must demonstrate financial need. Preference will be given to students whose combined household income does not exceed:

  • R350,000 per annum

Academic Performance

Students must have successfully passed at least:

  • 60% of their academic credits for the 2025 academic year

Degree Progression

Applicants should ideally be on track to complete their degree:

  • Within the minimum prescribed period
  • Or within n+1 years where applicable

Visa and Study Permit Requirements

Applicants must possess one of the following:

  • A valid study permit
  • Refugee documentation
  • Asylum seeker documentation
  • VFS receipt confirming a visa application

These documents must be valid for the full academic year.

Permanent Resident Applicants

Permanent residents may apply only if they do not qualify for NSFAS funding.

Important Conditions of the Bursary

Applicants should carefully note the following important conditions:

  • The bursary is not automatically renewable
  • Students must apply annually
  • Funding cannot be used to pay historical debt
  • Co-funding arrangements should be clearly indicated
  • Complete supporting documentation must be submitted before the deadline
  • Incomplete applications may not be considered

Required Application Documents

Applicants are strongly advised to upload all required supporting documents at the time of submission.

Failure to submit complete documentation may result in:

  • Delayed processing
  • Ineligibility
  • Disqualification of the application

Late submissions and requests for additional document uploads after submission will not be accepted.

Application Deadline

The application deadline for the 2026 Stellenbosch University SADC Undergraduate Bursary is:

Friday, 24 July 2026 at 16:00 (South African time)

Applicants are encouraged to apply early to avoid technical issues or incomplete submissions close to the deadline.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary represents a valuable opportunity for international students across Southern Africa who are pursuing higher education in South Africa.

With rising tuition and living costs, financial support programmes like this help talented students remain focused on their studies while reducing financial pressure.

The bursary also demonstrates Stellenbosch University’s continued commitment to supporting regional educational access, academic excellence, and student success across the African continent.

Students who meet the eligibility criteria are highly encouraged to prepare their documents early and submit strong applications before the deadline.


Learn more and apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary at Stellenbosch University in South Africa.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary at Stellenbosch University in South Africa | R50,000 Financial Support for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Groundbreaker Talents Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Software & AI Engineering Program for Young Women in Uganda
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Groundbreaker Talents has officially opened applications for its Cohort 7 Software & AI Engineering Scholarship Program 2026, a fully funded residential opportunity designed to empower young African women with industry-ready technology skills. The scholarship is specifically created for ambitious women from financially constrained backgrounds who want to build careers in Software Development and Artificial Intelligence […]

The post Groundbreaker Talents Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Software & AI Engineering Program for Young Women in Uganda appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Groundbreaker Talents has officially opened applications for its Cohort 7 Software & AI Engineering Scholarship Program 2026, a fully funded residential opportunity designed to empower young African women with industry-ready technology skills.

The scholarship is specifically created for ambitious women from financially constrained backgrounds who want to build careers in Software Development and Artificial Intelligence but may not have access to quality tech education opportunities.

Hosted at the Groundbreaker campus in Bukerere, Uganda, the 12-month residential training program equips participants with practical software engineering and AI skills, mentorship from global professionals, career guidance, healthcare support, and direct job placement assistance.

For young Sudanese women living in Uganda, refugees, and other Ugandan residents seeking a life-changing pathway into technology, this opportunity provides a fully sponsored route into the growing digital economy.

About the Groundbreaker Talents Program

Groundbreaker Talents is a social impact initiative focused on helping young African women launch sustainable careers in technology. Through immersive residential learning, mentorship, and real-world project experience, the organization aims to bridge the digital skills gap while creating employment opportunities for women across Africa.

Since 2023, the program has already achieved remarkable impact:

  • 153 talents trained
  • 98% employed within 3 months after graduation
  • 22× average income increase among graduates
  • 93% graduation rate
  • 19% employed in international companies

Participants live and learn together on campus throughout the one-year training experience, gaining not only technical knowledge but also personal development, professional exposure, and industry connections.

Scholarship Benefits

The Groundbreaker Talents Scholarship is fully funded and covers all major educational and living expenses throughout the duration of the program.

Selected participants will receive:

1. Full Software Development and AI Training

Students receive intensive training in:

  • Software Engineering
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Industry-relevant digital skills
  • Practical coding experience
  • Real-world tech projects

Training partners include:

  • Refactory Academy
  • Turing College

2. Free Laptop and Internet Access

Every participant receives:

  • A personal laptop
  • Internet connectivity for training and future remote work opportunities

3. Fully Covered Accommodation and Meals

Participants stay on the Groundbreaker NGO campus in Bukerere, Uganda, where:

  • Housing is fully provided
  • Daily meals are covered
  • Students learn in a collaborative residential environment

4. Mentorship from Global Tech Professionals

Students gain access to:

  • One-on-one mentorship sessions
  • Guidance from experienced international professionals
  • Career coaching and industry insights

5. Career Support and Job Placement

Groundbreaker Talents strongly emphasizes employability. Graduates receive:

  • Career readiness training
  • Job search support
  • Placement assistance
  • Networking opportunities with employers

According to the organization, 98% of graduates secure employment within three months after completing the program.

6. Healthcare and Mental Health Support

The program also provides:

  • Basic healthcare services
  • Mental health and wellness support
  • A safe and supportive learning environment

Who Can Apply?

Applicants must meet all the following eligibility requirements:

Age Requirement

  • Applicants must be women aged 18–30 at the time of application.

Residency Requirement

Applicants must be:

  • Ugandan residents, or
  • Refugees residing in Uganda

Educational Requirement

Applicants should have:

  • A credit in English at secondary school level
  • A credit in Mathematics at secondary school level

Language Requirement

  • Applicants must be proficient in English because all training is delivered in English.

Tech Background

  • No previous coding or technical experience is required.
  • Beginners are strongly encouraged to apply.

Commitment Requirement

Applicants must be willing to:

  • Commit full-time for one year
  • Live on campus throughout the program duration

Why This Scholarship Matters

Technology remains one of the fastest-growing career sectors globally, yet many young women across Africa face barriers accessing quality training, mentorship, and career opportunities.

The Groundbreaker Talents Scholarship addresses these challenges by:

  • Removing financial barriers
  • Providing fully funded education
  • Creating pathways into high-demand tech careers
  • Supporting women from underserved communities
  • Encouraging refugee inclusion in technology education

The program offers a rare opportunity for young women to transition from limited opportunities into professional software and AI careers with global potential.

Application Process

The application process consists of five stages:

Step 1: Online Application

Applicants must:

  • Complete the online application form
  • Upload required documents
  • Submit a 2–3 minute motivation video

Applicants are advised to compress large files before uploading to avoid technical submission issues.

Step 2: Phone Interview

Shortlisted applicants will receive:

  • An SMS invitation for a phone interview

Step 3: In-Person Interview

Selected candidates will then:

  • Visit the Groundbreaker campus
  • Attend a face-to-face interview with the Groundbreaker team

Step 4: Final Decision

All applicants will receive:

  • Notification via SMS or email regarding their application status

Step 5: Onboarding

Successful applicants will receive:

  • Onboarding instructions
  • Preparation guidance before the program begins

Alumni Success Stories

Groundbreaker Talents alumni have already gone on to secure professional roles in technology.

One alumna shared that the program enabled her to become financially independent and support her family and siblings through her salary as a software engineer.

Another graduate stated that she joined the campus with little knowledge of technology and later secured employment as a Software Engineer at the National Renewable Energy Platform.

These stories highlight the transformative impact of the program on young women’s lives and careers.

Important Notes

  • The application process is completely free.
  • No prior coding knowledge is required.
  • Refugees living in Uganda are eligible to apply.
  • The scholarship is fully residential and requires full-time commitment.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should apply through the official Groundbreaker Talents application portal.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare:

  • Academic documents
  • Identification documents
  • Motivation video
  • Contact information

Early application is highly recommended due to the competitive nature of the scholarship.

Deadline

The official application deadline has not yet been announced. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible while Cohort 7 applications remain open.

Learn more and apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Groundbreaker Talents Scholarship 2026.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Groundbreaker Talents Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Software & AI Engineering Program for Young Women in Uganda appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowships 2026: Fully Supported International Research Opportunity for Postdoctoral Researchers
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) has officially opened applications for the prestigious Postdoc.Mobility Fellowships 2026, a major international funding opportunity designed to support postdoctoral researchers seeking to strengthen their academic profile through research stays abroad. This fellowship programme is aimed at researchers who have completed their doctorate and want to deepen their expertise, expand […]

The post SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowships 2026: Fully Supported International Research Opportunity for Postdoctoral Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) has officially opened applications for the prestigious Postdoc.Mobility Fellowships 2026, a major international funding opportunity designed to support postdoctoral researchers seeking to strengthen their academic profile through research stays abroad.

This fellowship programme is aimed at researchers who have completed their doctorate and want to deepen their expertise, expand international collaborations, strengthen scientific independence, and accelerate their academic careers through global research experiences.

The SNSF Postdoc.Mobility programme is one of Switzerland’s leading initiatives for promoting international academic mobility and supporting outstanding early-career researchers across disciplines.

What is the SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowship?

The Postdoc.Mobility fellowship is a funding scheme established by the Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) to help postdoctoral researchers conduct research at institutions outside Switzerland.

The programme focuses on empowering researchers to:

  • Gain international research experience
  • Build strong academic networks abroad
  • Enhance scientific independence
  • Access world-class laboratories, archives, or research environments
  • Strengthen long-term academic and professional opportunities
  • Develop advanced expertise within their discipline

The fellowship encourages global scientific collaboration while helping Swiss-linked researchers become competitive leaders in research and innovation.

Fellowship Duration

The fellowship is generally awarded for:

  • Up to 24 months of funded research abroad
  • A minimum duration of 12 months

Researchers currently holding an ongoing Postdoc.Mobility fellowship may also apply for an additional return grant lasting between:

  • 3 to 12 months

The return grant supports researchers transitioning back after their international research stay.

Who Can Apply?

The SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowships are open to:

Eligible Applicants

  • Researchers who already hold a doctorate degree
  • Researchers expected to complete their doctorate within six months after the submission deadline
  • Researchers with a medical degree that includes a doctorate

Additional Requirement

Applicants must demonstrate a connection or link with Switzerland.

The programme is designed specifically for postdoctoral-level researchers seeking advanced international research opportunities.

Main Objective of the Programme

The core objective of the Postdoc.Mobility programme is to strengthen the next generation of researchers by providing them with opportunities to conduct high-quality research in international academic environments.

The fellowship aims to help participants:

  1. Build international scientific collaborations
  2. Improve academic visibility
  3. Expand interdisciplinary research experience
  4. Develop independent research leadership
  5. Access new research methods and infrastructure
  6. Increase competitiveness for future academic positions and grants

Benefits of the SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowship

Successful applicants may benefit from:

  • Funding support for international research stays
  • Long-term academic development opportunities
  • Enhanced scientific independence
  • Access to global research institutions and networks
  • International collaboration opportunities
  • Career advancement within academia and research sectors
  • Support for return grants after the fellowship period

The programme provides an important platform for researchers seeking to establish themselves internationally within the academic and scientific community.

Research Focus

The programme supports postdoctoral research stays abroad across academic and scientific disciplines.

Researchers can use the fellowship to:

  • Conduct independent research projects
  • Collaborate with international research groups
  • Access specialised facilities or expertise unavailable locally
  • Develop innovative research methodologies
  • Expand publication and academic partnership opportunities

About the Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF)

The Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) is Switzerland’s leading institution for the promotion of scientific research.

The organisation supports excellent research across multiple disciplines and provides funding opportunities for researchers at different career stages.

Through programmes like Postdoc.Mobility, SNSF continues to strengthen international scientific cooperation and support the development of emerging research leaders worldwide.

Why This Fellowship Matters

International research exposure has become increasingly important for academic career development. The SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowship provides researchers with an opportunity to gain valuable international experience while developing independent research capabilities.

For postdoctoral researchers aiming to build strong academic careers, this fellowship can serve as a major stepping stone toward future research leadership, institutional collaborations, and long-term scientific impact.

Researchers connected to Switzerland who are looking to expand their global research experience should strongly consider applying for this competitive and prestigious fellowship opportunity before the deadline.

Submission Deadline

Application Deadline:

4 August 2026 — 17:00 CEST

Applicants are encouraged to begin preparations early and carefully review all eligibility requirements and fellowship regulations before submission.

Learn more and apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowships 2026.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowships 2026: Fully Supported International Research Opportunity for Postdoctoral Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Nestlé Ghana National Service Program 2026: A Comprehensive Opportunity for Young Graduates to Build Professional Skills
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Nestlé, the world’s largest food and beverage company, continues its long-standing commitment to developing young talent through its National Service Program in Ghana. This 12‑month service opportunity is designed to equip fresh graduates with hands-on experience, professional exposure, and the chance to contribute meaningfully to impactful projects across the Central and West Africa Region (CWAR). […]

The post Nestlé Ghana National Service Program 2026: A Comprehensive Opportunity for Young Graduates to Build Professional Skills appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Nestlé, the world’s largest food and beverage company, continues its long-standing commitment to developing young talent through its National Service Program in Ghana. This 12‑month service opportunity is designed to equip fresh graduates with hands-on experience, professional exposure, and the chance to contribute meaningfully to impactful projects across the Central and West Africa Region (CWAR).

The program is based in Accra, Ghana, and offers a structured environment where young professionals can learn, grow, and build a strong foundation for their future careers.

About the Nestlé National Service Program

The National Service Person role at Nestlé is a full-time, one-year contract designed to support the company’s operations while nurturing the next generation of industry professionals. Participants join a dynamic, purpose-driven organization committed to enhancing the quality of life and contributing to a healthier future.

Nestlé’s work culture emphasizes respect, collaboration, innovation, and integrity—values that guide every aspect of the service experience.

Key Responsibilities of the National Service Person

Participants in this program will be actively involved in meaningful tasks that contribute to Nestlé’s operations. Responsibilities include:

  1. Supporting Daily Operations Assisting in routine activities within the assigned department to ensure smooth business processes.
  2. Project Support Contributing to ongoing projects through research, data analysis, reporting, and administrative duties.
  3. Cross-Functional Collaboration Working with multiple teams across the organization to support business objectives and operational efficiency.
  4. Training and Development Participation Engaging in Nestlé’s structured training programs, mentorship sessions, and learning modules designed to enhance professional capabilities.
  5. Creative and Strategic Contribution Bringing fresh ideas, innovative thinking, and proactive solutions to team discussions and project planning.
  6. Upholding Nestlé Values Demonstrating respect, integrity, collaboration, and innovation in all assigned tasks.

What Makes a Successful Candidate?

Nestlé seeks motivated, agile, and forward-thinking graduates who are eager to learn and contribute. Ideal candidates should possess:

  • A minimum of a Bachelor’s degree in:
    • Engineering
    • Social Sciences
    • Business Administration
    • IT or Computer Science
    • Graphic Design
    • Photography and Videography
  • OR an HND qualification in:
    • Mechanical Engineering
    • Electrical Engineering
    • Mechanical/Electrical Technician fields

Additional requirements include:

  • Completion of national service eligibility requirements as mandated by local legislation.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively.
  • Demonstrated leadership qualities and willingness to learn.
  • Analytical thinking and attention to detail.
  • Ability to speak French is an added advantage.

Why Join Nestlé?

Nestlé offers a unique environment where young professionals can thrive. As a global leader in the food and beverage industry, the company provides:

  • Exposure to real-world business operations
  • Opportunities to work on impactful projects
  • Access to mentorship and professional development
  • A diverse and inclusive workplace
  • A platform to build long-term career potential

Nestlé is an equal opportunity employer and encourages applicants from all backgrounds to apply.

Application Information

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early. Nestlé reviews applications carefully and contacts only shortlisted candidates for interviews.

Application Deadline: 23rd June 2026

Location: Accra, Ghana (PMB, KIA 32)

How to Apply

Applicants can explore available roles and submit their applications through Nestlé’s official career portal. The company emphasizes fairness, diversity, and equal opportunity throughout the recruitment process.

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more job opportunities here 

 

The post Nestlé Ghana National Service Program 2026: A Comprehensive Opportunity for Young Graduates to Build Professional Skills appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AAUW International Project Grants 2026: Up to $10,000 for Women Leaders Advancing Gender Equity Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Women leaders committed to creating meaningful change in their communities now have an opportunity to secure funding through the AAUW International Project Grants 2026. Designed exclusively for AAUW International Fellowship Alumnae, this grant program supports community-based initiatives that promote gender equity and improve opportunities for women and girls around the world. With grants of up […]

The post AAUW International Project Grants 2026: Up to $10,000 for Women Leaders Advancing Gender Equity Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Women leaders committed to creating meaningful change in their communities now have an opportunity to secure funding through the AAUW International Project Grants 2026. Designed exclusively for AAUW International Fellowship Alumnae, this grant program supports community-based initiatives that promote gender equity and improve opportunities for women and girls around the world.

With grants of up to $10,000, the program empowers former AAUW International Fellows to apply their academic expertise, leadership skills, and professional experience to projects that generate direct and lasting impact in their home countries.

The initiative reflects AAUW’s long-standing commitment to advancing equity for women and girls through education, leadership development, and community engagement.

About the AAUW International Project Grants

The American Association of University Women (AAUW) established the International Project Grants program to help former International Fellowship recipients transform their academic knowledge into practical solutions that address gender inequality.

The program recognizes that many AAUW alumnae return to their home countries equipped with advanced education, professional expertise, and leadership experience. Through these grants, AAUW provides financial support that enables these women to launch, strengthen, or expand projects designed to improve the lives of women and girls.

The grants support initiatives that:

  • Advance gender equity
  • Empower women and girls
  • Promote educational opportunities
  • Strengthen communities
  • Address social and economic inequalities
  • Create sustainable local impact
  • Support leadership development among women

Unlike traditional academic funding, these grants focus on practical, community-based interventions that generate measurable outcomes.

Grant Value

Successful applicants may receive funding of up to:

💰 Award Ceiling: $10,000

The grant funds are intended to support approved project activities during the grant period.

Recipients must maintain all grant funds in an account dedicated specifically to project purposes.

Purpose of the Grant

The International Project Grants aim to help AAUW alumnae:

  • Apply academic knowledge to real-world challenges
  • Expand opportunities for women and girls
  • Lead impactful community initiatives
  • Strengthen gender equality efforts
  • Promote social and economic empowerment
  • Build sustainable programs within their communities

Projects should demonstrate a clear commitment to advancing the status of women and girls within the applicant’s home country.

Who Can Apply?

The AAUW International Project Grants are awarded exclusively to individuals rather than organizations.

To qualify, applicants must meet all eligibility requirements.

Applicants Must:

1. Be or Identify as a Woman

The program is specifically designed to support women leaders and changemakers.

2. Be an AAUW International Fellowship Alumna

Applicants must have previously received an AAUW International Fellowship.

3. Have Successfully Completed Their Fellowship Program

Applicants must have:

  • Completed the academic program funded through their AAUW International Fellowship
  • Fulfilled all reporting requirements associated with the fellowship

These requirements must be completed by June 30 of the year of application.

4. Reside in Their Home Country

Applicants must live in their home country throughout the grant period.

5. Implement the Project in Their Home Country

The proposed initiative must be carried out within the applicant’s home country.

6. Serve as the Primary Project Director

Applicants must maintain full responsibility for:

  • Programmatic decisions
  • Budget management
  • Administrative oversight
  • Editorial decisions
  • Project implementation

AAUW expects grant recipients to have direct control over all major aspects of the project.

Citizenship and Immigration Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be non-U.S. citizens, and
  • Not hold U.S. permanent resident status

The program is intended specifically for international alumnae who are implementing projects outside the United States.

What Types of Projects Are Supported?

AAUW International Project Grants support community-based initiatives that contribute to gender equality and women’s empowerment.

Examples may include:

Education Initiatives

Projects that:

  • Increase girls’ access to education
  • Improve literacy rates
  • Support STEM education for girls
  • Provide vocational training opportunities

Economic Empowerment Programs

Initiatives focused on:

  • Entrepreneurship
  • Financial literacy
  • Women’s employment opportunities
  • Income-generation projects

Leadership Development

Programs that:

  • Build leadership skills
  • Mentor emerging women leaders
  • Promote civic participation
  • Strengthen women’s representation

Health and Well-Being

Projects addressing:

  • Women’s health education
  • Maternal health awareness
  • Mental health support
  • Community wellness initiatives

Gender Equality Advocacy

Programs designed to:

  • Raise awareness of gender issues
  • Reduce discrimination
  • Promote women’s rights
  • Support policy engagement

Applicants should clearly demonstrate how their proposed project will create measurable benefits for women and girls.

Selection Criteria

AAUW evaluates proposals based on several key factors.

Strong applications typically demonstrate:

Clear Impact

Applicants should explain:

  • Who will benefit
  • How the project will create change
  • Expected outcomes and results

Community Relevance

Projects should address genuine needs within the local context.

Feasibility

AAUW looks for projects that can realistically be implemented within the grant period and budget.

Leadership Capacity

Applicants should demonstrate their ability to successfully manage and execute the project.

Sustainability

Projects should show potential for continued impact beyond the grant period.

Grant Timeline

Application Period

📅 September 16, 2024 – November 22, 2024

Applications must be submitted during this period.

Award Notification

📅 April 15, 2025

Applicants will be informed of funding decisions.

Grant Fund Disbursement

📅 July 2025

Funding may be distributed in full or in part.

Grant Period

📅 July 1, 2025 – June 30, 2026

All project activities should be implemented during this timeframe.

Interim Project Report

📅 December 15, 2025 (if applicable)

Some grantees may be required to submit a mid-project progress report.

Final Impact Report Deadline

📅 July 31, 2026

Grant recipients must submit a final report outlining project achievements, outcomes, and impact.

Important Funding Rules

Dedicated Project Account

Grant funds must be maintained in a dedicated account used solely for project purposes.

Approved Use of Funds

Funds may only be used:

  • For approved project activities
  • Within the approved grant period
  • According to the finalized grant proposal

No Retroactive Funding

Grant funds cannot be used for:

  • Expenses incurred before the grant start date
  • Expenses incurred after the grant end date

unless prior written approval is obtained from AAUW.

Legal Compliance

Grantees must comply with:

  • Local laws
  • National regulations
  • International legal requirements
  • Relevant financial compliance standards

Tax Considerations

AAUW does not:

  • Withhold U.S. taxes from grants
  • Provide tax forms to grantees
  • Offer tax advice

Recipients are responsible for determining whether any tax obligations exist in their home countries.

Applicants are encouraged to consult local tax professionals regarding grant-related tax requirements.

Why This Grant Matters

The AAUW International Project Grants represent more than financial support.

They provide former fellows with the opportunity to:

  • Translate academic knowledge into action
  • Address pressing gender challenges
  • Strengthen communities
  • Advance women’s rights
  • Create sustainable local impact
  • Expand leadership influence

By supporting alumnae-led initiatives, AAUW continues to invest in women leaders who are creating meaningful change worldwide.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should carefully review:

  • Eligibility requirements
  • Application guidelines
  • Required documentation
  • Grant policies

Applications must be submitted through AAUW’s official grant application process during the designated application period.

Prospective applicants are encouraged to prepare their proposals well in advance to ensure they meet all requirements.

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The AAUW International Project Grants 2026 provide an exceptional opportunity for AAUW International Fellowship Alumnae to leverage their education, expertise, and leadership experience to improve the lives of women and girls in their home countries.

With funding of up to $10,000, the program empowers women leaders to implement impactful, community-based projects that address gender inequality and foster sustainable development.

For eligible alumnae seeking to create lasting change, this grant offers both the resources and recognition needed to turn ideas into meaningful action.

For more information on this grant/fellowship visit here

For more global grant/fellowship opportunities on OFY, visit here

The post AAUW International Project Grants 2026: Up to $10,000 for Women Leaders Advancing Gender Equity Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Study, Research, and Build Global Networks in the United States
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026, one of the most respected international scholarship programs in the world. The scholarship provides Australian students, researchers, professionals, and scholars with the opportunity to undertake study, research, teaching, or professional development in the United States while building long-term academic, professional, and cultural connections. Established […]

The post Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Study, Research, and Build Global Networks in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026, one of the most respected international scholarship programs in the world. The scholarship provides Australian students, researchers, professionals, and scholars with the opportunity to undertake study, research, teaching, or professional development in the United States while building long-term academic, professional, and cultural connections.

Established to strengthen mutual understanding between Australia and the United States, the Fulbright Program has transformed the careers of thousands of scholars by providing access to leading universities, world-class research institutions, and influential professional networks.

Whether you are pursuing postgraduate studies, conducting advanced research, developing innovative solutions to global challenges, or expanding your professional expertise, the Fulbright Scholarship offers substantial financial support and a life-changing international experience.

About the Fulbright Scholarship Program

The Fulbright Program was established in Australia in 1949 through a binational treaty between the Australian and United States Governments, inspired by the vision of U.S. Senator J. William Fulbright.

Today, the program is administered by the Australian-American Fulbright Commission and remains one of the world’s most prestigious international exchange initiatives.

The mission of the Fulbright Program is to promote:

  • Educational exchange
  • Research collaboration
  • Cultural understanding
  • Academic excellence
  • Professional development
  • Long-term partnerships between Australia and the United States

Over the decades, Fulbright alumni have gone on to become leaders in government, academia, science, business, technology, healthcare, arts, and international affairs.

Why the Fulbright Scholarship Is So Prestigious

The Fulbright Scholarship is far more than a funding opportunity.

It is a globally recognized program that provides scholars with:

  • Access to leading U.S. universities
  • International research collaborations
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Leadership development
  • Cultural exchange experiences
  • Global recognition and credibility
  • Lifelong membership in the Fulbright alumni network

The program emphasizes not only academic achievement but also the ability of scholars to act as ambassadors who strengthen relationships between nations.

Scholarship Benefits

Fulbright Scholarships provide extensive financial and professional support.

Benefits vary depending on the specific award category and level of study, but generally include:

Monthly Living Stipend

Recipients receive a monthly stipend based on the cost of living in the city where their host institution is located.

This support helps cover:

  • Accommodation
  • Meals
  • Transportation
  • Daily living expenses

Tuition Support

Depending on the scholarship category, recipients may receive:

  • Partial tuition support for one academic year, or
  • Full tuition support under selected awards such as the Fulbright Future Award

Round-Trip International Airfare

The scholarship covers:

✈️ Economy-class return airfare

from the scholar’s home city in Australia to the host institution in the United States.

Establishment Allowance

Recipients receive financial assistance to help settle into their new academic environment upon arrival.

Dependent Allowance

Where applicable, additional support may be available for eligible dependents accompanying the scholar.

Placement Assistance

Graduate coursework applicants may receive assistance in identifying suitable U.S. host institutions through Fulbright Placement Services.

Comprehensive Health and Accident Insurance

The scholarship includes:

🏥 Health and Accident Insurance (ASPE)

Valued at up to:

USD $100,000

for the scholar.

Professional Networks

Scholars gain access to:

  • Fulbright alumni networks
  • Academic experts
  • International collaborators
  • Professional organizations
  • Research communities

Orientation and Enrichment Programs

Participants benefit from:

  • Pre-departure orientation in Australia
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Cultural enrichment activities
  • U.S.-based Fulbright events

Ongoing Support

The Fulbright Commission provides extensive support throughout the scholarship journey.

This includes:

  • Application guidance
  • Program coordination
  • Administrative support
  • Professional networking opportunities

Media and Publicity Opportunities

Recipients may also receive support in promoting their work and achievements through Fulbright communication channels.

Scholarship Duration

📅 One year or more

The duration varies depending on the award category and academic program.

Number of Scholarships Available

🎓 Approximately 10 scholarships are available.

Who Can Apply?

The Fulbright Scholarship supports a broad range of applicants.

Eligible categories include:

Postgraduate Students

Students pursuing:

  • Graduate coursework programs
  • Graduate research degrees

Postdoctoral Scholars

Researchers seeking advanced academic and research opportunities.

Professional Scholars

Professionals interested in developing expertise and strengthening international collaboration.

Senior Scholars

Experienced academics and researchers seeking to advance knowledge and international partnerships.

Eligible Study Levels

Applicants may apply under:

  • Graduate Coursework
  • Graduate Research

Eligible Applicant Types

Applications are open to:

  • Future students
  • Current students
  • Alumni

Fields of Study

One of the strengths of the Fulbright Program is its openness to all academic disciplines.

Eligible study areas include:

Agriculture and Veterinary Sciences

  • Agricultural innovation
  • Food systems
  • Animal health
  • Sustainability

Architecture, Building, Planning and Design

  • Urban development
  • Sustainable design
  • Smart cities

Arts, Humanities and Social Sciences

  • Cultural studies
  • Public policy
  • History
  • Sociology
  • International affairs

Business and Economics

  • Entrepreneurship
  • Finance
  • Economic development
  • Leadership

Education

  • Educational leadership
  • Learning innovation
  • Curriculum development

Engineering

  • Civil engineering
  • Mechanical engineering
  • Electrical engineering
  • Emerging technologies

Environment

  • Climate change
  • Environmental management
  • Sustainability

Health

  • Public health
  • Medical research
  • Health systems strengthening

Information Technology and Computer Science

  • Artificial intelligence
  • Cybersecurity
  • Data science
  • Software development

Law

  • International law
  • Human rights
  • Public policy

Music, Visual and Performing Arts

  • Creative practice
  • Arts management
  • Cultural exchange

Science

  • Natural sciences
  • Physical sciences
  • Applied sciences

Selection Criteria

Fulbright selection panels evaluate applicants based on several important attributes.

Academic and Professional Excellence

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Outstanding academic achievement
  • Strong professional accomplishments
  • Evidence of leadership potential

Strong Program Proposal

Successful candidates present:

  • Clear objectives
  • Well-developed project plans
  • Meaningful research or study goals

Potential Impact

Applicants should explain:

  • Expected outcomes
  • Long-term benefits
  • Broader societal contributions

Ambassadorial Qualities

The Fulbright Program values candidates who can:

  • Build international relationships
  • Represent Australia positively
  • Promote cross-cultural understanding
  • Foster collaboration between nations

Why You Should Apply

The Fulbright Scholarship is widely regarded as one of the most transformative international opportunities available to Australian scholars.

Benefits include:

✅ World-class education and research opportunities

✅ Fully funded support for study in the United States

✅ Access to prestigious academic institutions

✅ International professional networks

✅ Leadership development opportunities

✅ Enhanced career prospects

✅ Lifelong Fulbright alumni connections

For many recipients, the Fulbright experience becomes a defining milestone in their academic and professional journey.

Information Session

Prospective applicants can learn more through the official Fulbright information webinar.

Webinar Details

📅 Date: 12 May 2026

⏰ Time: 1:00 PM – 2:00 PM AEST

The session provides valuable insights into the application process, scholarship categories, and selection criteria.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted directly through the Fulbright Australia application system.

Applicants should carefully review all eligibility requirements and prepare their application materials well in advance of the deadline.

Apply Here 

Important Dates

Applications Open

📅 3 March 2026

Applications Close

📅 1 July 2026

Late applications are unlikely to be considered.

Final Thoughts

The Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026 offers a remarkable opportunity for Australian scholars to study, research, and collaborate in the United States while becoming part of one of the world’s most influential academic and professional networks.

With generous financial support, access to leading institutions, and unparalleled opportunities for international engagement, the Fulbright Program continues to empower future leaders who are committed to creating positive impact at local, national, and global levels.

If you aspire to expand your knowledge, strengthen international partnerships, and contribute meaningfully to your field, the Fulbright Scholarship may be the opportunity that transforms your future.

For more information on this scholarship visit here 

For more fully funded scholarships on OFY, visit here

The post Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Study, Research, and Build Global Networks in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ISS Dakar Junior Fellow Opportunity 2026 in Senegal: Fully Funded Research Fellowship for Young Professionals in Peace and Security
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Institute for Security Studies (ISS) is inviting applications from talented young Senegalese researchers for its Junior Fellow position based in Dakar, Senegal. This prestigious 12-month fellowship offers a unique opportunity for early-career professionals to gain hands-on experience in peace and security research while working with one of Africa’s leading policy and research institutions. The […]

The post ISS Dakar Junior Fellow Opportunity 2026 in Senegal: Fully Funded Research Fellowship for Young Professionals in Peace and Security appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Institute for Security Studies (ISS) is inviting applications from talented young Senegalese researchers for its Junior Fellow position based in Dakar, Senegal. This prestigious 12-month fellowship offers a unique opportunity for early-career professionals to gain hands-on experience in peace and security research while working with one of Africa’s leading policy and research institutions.

The selected Junior Fellow will support the ISS Regional Office for West Africa and the Sahel by conducting research, contributing to policy analysis, organizing events, and monitoring political and security developments across the region. The fellowship provides an excellent platform for young researchers interested in governance, peacebuilding, conflict prevention, terrorism, violent extremism, and international affairs.

For aspiring policy analysts, researchers, and development professionals seeking to build a career in African peace and security studies, this opportunity offers valuable professional experience, mentorship, and exposure to regional and international policy discussions.

About the Institute for Security Studies (ISS)

Institute for Security Studies is one of Africa’s most respected independent research institutions dedicated to advancing human security, peace, governance, and sustainable development across the continent.

The organization works with governments, regional institutions, policymakers, civil society organizations, and international partners to:

  • Strengthen governance systems
  • Promote peace and stability
  • Prevent conflict and violence
  • Support evidence-based policymaking
  • Enhance regional security cooperation
  • Advance democratic institutions

Through its research, policy analysis, training programs, and stakeholder engagement initiatives, ISS contributes significantly to shaping policy responses to Africa’s most pressing security challenges.

About the Junior Fellow Position

The ISS Dakar Junior Fellow position is designed to provide practical research experience and professional development opportunities to young researchers interested in West African peace and security issues.

The successful candidate will join the ISS Regional Office for West Africa and the Sahel and work directly under the supervision of a Senior Researcher.

This fellowship combines:

  • Research experience
  • Policy analysis
  • Event coordination
  • Professional writing
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Regional security monitoring

The position offers a unique opportunity to contribute to meaningful policy discussions while developing critical analytical and research skills.

Fellowship Location

📍 Dakar, Senegal

The successful candidate will be based at the ISS Dakar office throughout the duration of the fellowship.

Dakar serves as an important political, diplomatic, and policy hub in West Africa, offering fellows exposure to regional institutions, international organizations, government actors, and development partners working across the region.

Fellowship Duration

Start Date

20 July 2026

Contract Length

12 Months

The selected fellow will work full-time during the fellowship period.

What Will the Junior Fellow Do?

The Junior Fellow will support the ISS research team across a range of activities.

1. Conduct Background Research

The fellow will:

  • Gather information on regional security developments
  • Review relevant literature and policy documents
  • Support ongoing research projects
  • Assist in data collection and analysis

2. Undertake Desk and Field Research

Under the guidance of a Senior Researcher, the fellow will:

  • Conduct independent research
  • Collect qualitative and quantitative information
  • Participate in field missions where required
  • Contribute to policy-relevant research outputs

3. Support Event Organization

The fellow will help organize:

  • Workshops
  • Policy dialogues
  • Seminars
  • Conferences
  • Stakeholder consultations

These events may take place in Dakar and selected countries across the region.

4. Attend Conferences and Seminars

Responsibilities include:

  • Participating in policy discussions
  • Taking detailed notes
  • Preparing event reports
  • Summarizing key findings and recommendations

5. Prepare Meeting Documentation

The fellow will:

  • Record meeting discussions
  • Draft meeting minutes
  • Support documentation processes
  • Maintain organized records

6. Monitor Political and Security Developments

An important responsibility includes:

  • Tracking developments across West Africa and the Sahel
  • Preparing weekly political and security reviews
  • Presenting findings to the research team
  • Identifying emerging trends and risks

7. Write Policy Articles

The selected candidate will:

  • Draft short analytical articles
  • Contribute to ISS publications
  • Develop policy-focused content
  • Improve research communication skills

All writing activities will be conducted under the supervision of a Senior Researcher.

8. Support Additional Project Activities

The fellow may also undertake other project-related tasks assigned by the Senior Researcher.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for:

  • Recent master’s graduates
  • Early-career researchers
  • Policy analysts
  • International relations graduates
  • Social science researchers
  • Governance specialists
  • Peacebuilding practitioners
  • Security studies enthusiasts

Applicants should have a strong interest in:

  • Peace and security
  • Governance
  • Conflict prevention
  • Terrorism studies
  • Violent extremism
  • Regional cooperation
  • Political analysis

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for this fellowship, applicants must meet the following criteria.

Nationality Requirement

Applicants must be:

✅ Nationals of Senegal

This opportunity is not open to applicants from other countries.

Age Requirement

Applicants must be:

✅ Under 30 years of age

Educational Requirement

Applicants must hold a:

✅ Master’s Degree

in one of the following fields:

  • Anthropology
  • Sociology
  • Politics
  • Law
  • History
  • International Relations
  • Related disciplines

Language Requirements

Applicants must be fluent in:

Either

  • English

or

  • French

Both written and spoken proficiency are required.

Additional Advantage

Knowledge of one or more local languages will be considered an asset.

Required Skills and Competencies

Successful candidates should demonstrate:

Research Skills

  • Literature review
  • Data collection
  • Policy analysis
  • Report writing
  • Critical thinking

Organizational Skills

  • Time management
  • Planning and coordination
  • Attention to detail
  • Reliability

Communication Skills

  • Strong writing abilities
  • Presentation skills
  • Professional communication
  • Team collaboration

Technical Skills

Applicants should be proficient in:

  • Microsoft Word
  • Microsoft Excel
  • Microsoft PowerPoint
  • Internet research tools
  • Prezi

Personal Qualities

The ISS seeks candidates who are:

  • Self-motivated
  • Curious
  • Adaptable
  • Professional
  • Responsible
  • Able to work under pressure

Fellowship Benefits

Monthly Stipend

The selected Junior Fellow will receive a monthly stipend throughout the fellowship period.

Professional Development

Participants will gain:

  • Practical policy research experience
  • Exposure to regional security issues
  • Mentorship from senior researchers
  • Publication opportunities
  • Networking opportunities
  • Event management experience

Career Advancement

The fellowship provides valuable experience for careers in:

  • Research institutions
  • International organizations
  • Government agencies
  • Think tanks
  • Development organizations
  • Academic institutions

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

The Institute for Security Studies is committed to promoting employment equity and inclusive participation.

The organization particularly encourages:

👩 Female applicants to apply.

ISS values diversity and seeks to create an inclusive work environment where individuals from different backgrounds can contribute meaningfully to its mission.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their application via email.

Application Email

dakarjobs@issafrica.org

Contact Person

Souleymane Ndiaye

Apply Here 

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

1. Cover Letter

Clearly explaining:

  • Motivation for applying
  • Relevant experience
  • Interest in peace and security research

2. Detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Including:

  • Educational background
  • Professional experience
  • Research experience
  • Skills and competencies

3. Referees

Provide at least:

  • Three contactable referees

4. Identity Document

A copy of a valid identity document must be included.

Selection Process

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further stages of the recruitment process.

Applicants are encouraged to submit complete and well-prepared applications before the deadline.

Application Deadline

19 June 2026

Late submissions may not be considered.

Why You Should Apply

The ISS Dakar Junior Fellow opportunity offers a rare chance for young Senegalese professionals to build a strong foundation in policy research and security analysis while contributing to important conversations shaping the future of West Africa and the Sahel.

By joining ISS, fellows will gain practical experience, professional mentorship, and exposure to regional policy processes that can significantly strengthen their future academic and professional careers.

For young researchers passionate about peacebuilding, governance, security, and public policy, this fellowship represents an outstanding stepping stone into Africa’s policy and research landscape.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post ISS Dakar Junior Fellow Opportunity 2026 in Senegal: Fully Funded Research Fellowship for Young Professionals in Peace and Security appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Northern Illinois University Political Science Graduate Programs 2026: Fully Funded Master’s and Doctoral Opportunities in the United States
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Students interested in advancing their academic and professional careers in political science now have an excellent opportunity to apply for graduate studies at Northern Illinois University (NIU). Through its Department of Political Science within the College of Liberal Arts and Sciences, NIU offers Master of Arts (M.A.) and Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) programs designed to […]

The post Northern Illinois University Political Science Graduate Programs 2026: Fully Funded Master’s and Doctoral Opportunities in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students interested in advancing their academic and professional careers in political science now have an excellent opportunity to apply for graduate studies at Northern Illinois University (NIU). Through its Department of Political Science within the College of Liberal Arts and Sciences, NIU offers Master of Arts (M.A.) and Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) programs designed to prepare students for leadership roles in academia, government, public policy, international affairs, law, research, and many other professional fields.

The university provides a supportive academic environment, distinguished faculty mentorship, and competitive funding opportunities for qualified graduate students. Applicants can pursue specialized studies across several major fields of political science while benefiting from assistantships, fellowships, tuition waivers, and stipends.

Application Deadline

January 15

While applications may sometimes be considered after the deadline, prospective students are strongly encouraged to apply before January 15. Applicants wishing to submit materials after the deadline should contact the department directly before applying.

Why Study Political Science at Northern Illinois University?

Northern Illinois University’s Department of Political Science offers an advanced educational experience that equips students with the analytical, research, and professional skills needed to excel in a wide range of careers.

The department consists of:

  • Approximately 12 distinguished faculty members.
  • Around 45 graduate students.
  • A collaborative and supportive academic community.
  • Opportunities for research, teaching, and professional development.

Graduate students work closely with faculty mentors and fellow scholars while pursuing their academic and professional goals.

Areas of Specialization

Students can tailor their graduate studies based on their interests by selecting one of the department’s major areas of study.

1. American Government and Public Law

This concentration focuses on:

  • American political institutions.
  • Constitutional law.
  • Public policy.
  • Judicial processes.
  • Political behavior.
  • Governance and administration.

2. Comparative Politics

Students explore:

  • Political systems around the world.
  • Democratization and political development.
  • Comparative institutions.
  • Political transitions.
  • Governance across different countries and regions.

3. International Relations

This specialization examines:

  • Global politics.
  • International security.
  • Foreign policy.
  • International organizations.
  • Diplomacy.
  • Global governance.

4. Political Theory

Students engage with:

  • Classical political thought.
  • Contemporary political philosophy.
  • Democratic theory.
  • Ethics and governance.
  • Normative approaches to political analysis.

Graduate Degree Options

Master of Arts (M.A.) in Political Science

The M.A. program is designed for students seeking advanced political science training and can serve as preparation for:

  • Doctoral studies.
  • Public service careers.
  • Policy analysis.
  • Government positions.
  • Research roles.
  • Nonprofit and international organizations.

Expected Completion Time

Students can typically complete the Master of Arts degree within:

  • Two years.
  • Four semesters of full-time study.

Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) in Political Science

The Ph.D. program provides advanced scholarly training for students interested in academic, research, and leadership careers.

Completion Timeline for Students with a Master’s Degree

Students entering with an existing M.A. can typically complete the Ph.D. within:

  • Five to six semesters.

Completion Timeline for Students without a Master’s Degree

Students entering directly into the doctoral pathway can generally complete the program within:

  • Five years.
  • Ten semesters.

Students may complete Master’s degree requirements while progressing toward the Ph.D.

Graduate Funding Opportunities

One of the most attractive features of the Northern Illinois University Political Science graduate program is the availability of funding support for qualified students.

Funding is awarded competitively and is available to applicants who maintain strong academic standing.

The department also collaborates with other university units, including specialized centers and programs, to help secure additional funding opportunities.

Available funding may include:

  • Graduate assistantships.
  • Fellowships.
  • Tuition waivers.
  • Research support opportunities.

Graduate Assistantship Expectations

Students who receive assistantships and tuition waivers are generally expected to:

  • Enroll full time.
  • Work with graduate faculty members.
  • Perform assigned academic duties.
  • Work no more than 20 hours per week.

These assistantships provide valuable professional experience while helping students finance their education.

Funding for Master’s Degree Students

Students pursuing the Master of Arts degree can typically expect:

  • Up to two years of graduate assistantship support.
  • Financial assistance during fall and spring semesters.
  • A graduate stipend.
  • A tuition waiver covering most tuition-related expenses.

This funding significantly reduces educational costs while allowing students to focus on their academic goals.

Funding for Doctoral Students

Doctoral students may receive:

  • Up to five years of funding support.
  • Graduate assistantships.
  • Tuition-related support.
  • Professional development opportunities.

The department indicates that the minimum Ph.D. assistantship award is approximately:

$14,000 per academic year

Applicants may contact the department for the most current stipend information.

Career Opportunities After Graduation

Graduates of Northern Illinois University’s Political Science programs have successfully established careers across numerous disciplines and sectors.

Potential career paths include:

  • University teaching and research.
  • Government administration.
  • Public policy analysis.
  • International affairs.
  • Diplomacy.
  • Public law and legal professions.
  • Political consulting.
  • Nonprofit leadership.
  • Public service organizations.
  • Think tanks and research institutions.

The program’s broad academic training enables graduates to pursue diverse professional opportunities both within and beyond academia.

Supportive Academic Community

Graduate students benefit from:

  • Close faculty mentorship.
  • Research collaboration opportunities.
  • Professional networking.
  • Interdisciplinary engagement.
  • Academic conferences and scholarly activities.
  • A welcoming graduate student community.

The department’s relatively small size allows students to receive personalized attention and guidance throughout their studies.

How to Apply

Prospective students should submit an application for admission to the Northern Illinois University Political Science graduate program.

Applicants are encouraged to:

  1. Review graduate admission requirements.
  2. Prepare application materials early.
  3. Submit applications before January 15.
  4. Explore available funding opportunities.
  5. Contact the department with questions regarding admissions or assistantships.

Early application is highly recommended due to the competitive nature of funding awards.

APPLY HERE

Final Thoughts

Northern Illinois University’s Political Science graduate programs offer an excellent opportunity for students seeking advanced education, specialized training, and financial support in the field of political science. With strong faculty mentorship, multiple areas of specialization, generous funding opportunities, and pathways to diverse career outcomes, the program provides a solid foundation for future scholars, policymakers, researchers, and public service professionals.

Students interested in pursuing a fully funded graduate education in the United States should strongly consider applying before the January 15 deadline to maximize their chances of admission and funding consideration.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.
